authorities.name: U.S. Geological Survey (Geographic Names Information System)

sources.title: State of Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners — CR 25-16: Fishing Seasons and Regulations, January 1, 2026 – December 31, 2027
sources.quotedText: Pahranagat National Wildlife Refuge: Open year around except North Marsh which is closed to fishing during the waterfowl season. Daily: 3 trout and 15 warmwater game fish of which not more than 5 may be black bass. Possession: 6 trout and 30 warmwater game fish of which not more than 10 may be black bass. Hours: Any hour of the day or night.

sources.title: Fishing at Pahranagat National Wildlife Refuge — U.S. Fish & Wildlife Service
Pahranagat Lake, Upper — Nevada Department of Wildlife
sources.quotedText: Currently, carp and bullhead are the most common fish in Upper Pahranagat Lake... a few largemouth bass do exist.

sources.title: Apply & Buy — Nevada Fishing Licenses — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map) — Nevada Domestic Names bulk data, record for feature_id 858225, "Upper Pahranagat Lake", Reservoir, Lincoln Co., NV
sources.quotedText: 858225|Upper Pahranagat Lake|Reservoir|Nevada|32|Lincoln|017|Alamo|12/12/1980|03/18/2019||||371759N|1150734W|37.2997617|-115.1262275|||0.0|0.0 — official primary coordinates lat 37.2997617, lon -115.1262275 (the carto.nationalmap.gov ArcGIS geonames mirror does not currently return this record by name or gaz_id, so the authoritative bulk GNIS text file was used instead); corrects the file's previous guessed coordinate (-115.1225, 37.2866), which was roughly 1.5 km off.

parameters.note: Open year-round refuge-wide, except North Marsh, which closes to fishing during Nevada's waterfowl season (see closure rule).

regulations.humanSummary: Pahranagat National Wildlife Refuge waters are open to fishing year-round, except North Marsh, which is closed to fishing during the waterfowl season.
regulations.verbatimText: Open year around except North Marsh which is closed to fishing during the waterfowl season.

parameters.note: North Marsh (a distinct sub-unit of the refuge) is closed to fishing during Nevada's waterfowl hunting season. FWS separately posts North Marsh as closed to fishing October 1 through February 1 and closed to boating/floatation devices year-round; the CR 25-16 closure tracks the waterfowl season, which NDOW/NBWC sets annually (typically early October through late January).

regulations.humanSummary: North Marsh, within the refuge, is closed to fishing during the waterfowl season.
regulations.verbatimText: Open year around except North Marsh which is closed to fishing during the waterfowl season.

parameters.counting_rule: Daily limit is 3 trout plus 15 warmwater game fish, of which not more than 5 may be black bass. Possession limit is 6 trout plus 30 warmwater game fish, of which not more than 10 may be black bass.

regulations.humanSummary: Daily limit: 3 trout and 15 warmwater game fish (no more than 5 black bass). Possession limit: 6 trout and 30 warmwater game fish (no more than 10 black bass).
regulations.verbatimText: Daily: 3 trout and 15 warmwater game fish of which not more than 5 may be black bass. Possession: 6 trout and 30 warmwater game fish of which not more than 10 may be black bass.

parameters.note: No hour-of-day restriction; fishing permitted any hour of the day or night.

regulations.humanSummary: Fishing is permitted any hour of the day or night.
regulations.verbatimText: Hours: Any hour of the day or night.

parameters.note: Standard Nevada resident/nonresident fishing license required for anglers 12 and older, plus compliance with all applicable Refuge/FWS access rules.

regulations.humanSummary: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers age 12 and older; no license required under age 12.
regulations.verbatimText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.
